opinion.h4: CHAVEZ v. CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE DISTRICT COURT OF APPEAL OF CALIFORNIA, SECOND APPELLATE DISTRICT. No. 1113, Misc. Decided June 17, 1963.
opinion.p: Reported below: 208 Cal. App. 2d 248, 24 Cal. Rptr. 895.
The appeal is dismissed. Treating the papers whereon the appeal was taken as a petition for writ of certiorari, certiorari is denied.
374 U.S. 493 TRAUB v. CONNECTICUT. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE SUPREME COURT OF ERRORS OF CONNECTICUT. No. 1285, Misc. Decided June 17, 1963.
Certiorari granted; judgment vacated; and case remanded.
John D. LaBelle and Harry W. Hultgren, Jr. for respondent.

The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded for further consideration in light of Wong Sun v. United States, 371 U.S. 471, and Ker v. California, ante, p. 23.
